questions.question: Explain the working of SMTP protocol in email communication.
keyPoints: SMTP uses TCP port 25 for server-to-server and port 587 for client-to-server communication
It follows a command-response sequence: HELO/EHLO, MAIL FROM, RCPT TO, DATA, QUIT
Messages are transferred in plain text format between MTAs
SMTP supports authentication mechanisms for secure submission

questions.question: Differentiate between POP3 and IMAP protocols.
keyPoints: POP3 downloads emails and optionally deletes from server; IMAP keeps emails on server
IMAP allows multi-device access and synchronization of folders
IMAP supports partial fetch and server-side search
POP3 is simpler with offline capability; IMAP is better for always-connected devices

questions.question: Describe the components of an email system architecture.
keyPoints: MUA (Mail User Agent): Email client for composing/receiving emails
MTA (Mail Transfer Agent): Routes emails between servers using SMTP
MDA (Mail Delivery Agent): Delivers emails to recipient's mailbox
MRA (Mail Retrieval Agent): Retrieves emails from server to client

questions.question: Explain MIME (Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions) and its need.
keyPoints: MIME extends email capability beyond plain text to support multimedia
It defines content types for images, audio, video, applications
MIME encodes binary data using Base64 for text-based email transmission
Headers include Content-Type, Content-Transfer-Encoding, Content-Disposition

questions.question: What are the common email security threats and their prevention methods?
keyPoints: Phishing: Use anti-phishing filters, verify sender, don't click suspicious links
Spam: Use spam filters, don't reveal email address publicly
Email Spoofing: Implement SPF, DKIM, DMARC authentication
Use SSL/TLS encryption for transmission, PGP/S/MIME for end-to-end security

questions.question: Explain the structure of an email header with an example.
keyPoints: From: Sender's email address
To: Primary recipient(s)
CC: Carbon copy recipients
Subject: Email topic summary
Date: Timestamp of sending
Message-ID: Unique identifier
Received: Path showing servers traversed

questions.question: Describe PGP (Pretty Good Privacy) for email security.
keyPoints: PGP uses public-key cryptography for email encryption
Sender encrypts with recipient's public key; only recipient can decrypt with private key
PGP also provides digital signatures for authentication and integrity
Uses web of trust model for key verification instead of centralized CA

questions.question: Draw and explain the architecture of an electronic mail system showing all components and their interactions.
keyPoints: Diagram showing: MUA → MRA → MTA → Internet → MTA → MDA → MUA
Explain flow: User composes email in MUA, MRA forwards to MTA using SMTP
MTA transfers email across network to recipient's MTA
MDA delivers to recipient's mailbox
MRA retrieves email for recipient using POP3/IMAP
Show protocols: SMTP for transfer, POP3/IMAP for retrieval
Mention security layer (SSL/TLS) between components

questions.question: Explain the SMTP protocol operations with the state diagram and command sequence.
keyPoints: SMTP uses TCP port 25/587 with reliable connection
Connection establishment: Client connects to server port 25
State diagram: Initial → Mail Transactions → Final
Commands: EHLO/HELO, AUTH LOGIN, MAIL FROM, RCPT TO, DATA, QUIT
Responses: 2xx success, 4xx temporary failure, 5xx permanent failure
Example sequence with actual response codes
Mention ESMTP extensions like SIZE, STARTTLS

questions.question: Explain email message format according to RFC 822. Also describe how MIME extends this format.
keyPoints: RFC 822 defines header fields and body separated by blank line
Essential headers: From, To, Subject, Date, Message-ID
Optional headers: CC, BCC, Reply-To, In-Reply-To, References
MIME adds: Content-Type, Content-Transfer-Encoding, Content-Disposition
MIME types: text/plain, text/html, image/jpeg, audio/mp3, video/mp4
Boundary parameter for multipart messages
Base64 encoding for binary data
Example multipart MIME message structure

questions.question: Discuss the various email security mechanisms used to protect email communication.
keyPoints: Transport layer: SSL/TLS encryption for SMTP, POP3, IMAP
Authentication: SPF (Sender Policy Framework) validates sending server
DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail) adds cryptographic signature
DMARC provides policy for handling authentication failures
End-to-end encryption: PGP uses public-key with web of trust
S/MIME uses certificates for encryption and digital signatures
Anti-spam/anti-phishing: Content filters, blacklists, machine learning
